faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: How much does it cost to launch a campaign?
faq.a1: Minimum budget 3,000 FCFA. You pay 50 FCFA per verified click, only when a real visitor clicks your link.
faq.q2: How do I get paid as an Echo?
faq.a2: Directly to your Wave account. Request a withdrawal from the app, minimum 500 FCFA.
faq.q3: How do I know the clicks are real?
faq.a3: Every click is validated server-side: IP verification, bot detection, device fingerprinting. You only pay for clean clicks.
faq.a4: Yes. Free signup, no subscription, no fees. You earn 50 FCFA for every click your Status generates.
faq.q5: Can I target a specific city?
faq.a5: Tamtam covers 40+ cities. You can target by geographic area when creating your campaign.
faq.q6: How much can you earn per month as an Echo?
faq.a6: It depends on your number of WhatsApp contacts and consistency. Some Echos earn 5,000–15,000 FCFA/month.

support.title: Support
support.messageSent: Your message has been sent. We'll respond as soon as possible.
support.subject: Subject *
support.faq1q: How does cost per click work?
support.faq1a: You set a price per click (CPC). Your budget is deducted for each verified click. You only pay for real visitors.
support.faq2a: Our system verifies every click. Duplicates, bots, and suspicious clicks are automatically filtered to protect your budget.
support.faq3q: How do I recharge my wallet?
support.faq3a: Go to Wallet → Recharge. Pay via Wave. Your balance is credited after validation.
support.faq4q: How long does a campaign last?
support.faq4a: Until your budget is consumed. Most campaigns are completed in 2-5 days.
support.faq5a: Yes! Go to Rythmes → select your campaign → Pause. You can reactivate it at any time.
support.whatsappSupportDesc: Need quick help? Contact us on WhatsApp.
support.faq6q: How do I pause or reactivate a rythme?
support.faq6a: Open your active rythme and click "Pause". Unspent budget is refunded to your wallet. To resume, click "Reactivate" — the remaining budget will be debited again.

brandPage.faq1q: How does cost per click work?
brandPage.faq1a: You set a CPC for your campaign. You only pay when a visitor actually clicks your link. Clicks are verified by our anti-fraud system.
brandPage.faq2q: How long does a campaign last?
brandPage.faq2a: A campaign runs as long as your budget is active. You can pause or stop it at any time.

brandPage.faq4q: How do I top up my wallet?
brandPage.faq4a: You can top up via Wave or Orange Money directly from your dashboard.
brandPage.faq5a: Yes. Our system verifies each click with anti-fraud mechanisms: unique IP, minimum delay, browser fingerprint.

hero.desc: The Tamtam Pixel is a lightweight server-side tracker. Verified clicks, signups, activations, purchases — everything flows into your dashboard in real time.

quickstart.title: Integration in 5 minutes
quickstart.step2Desc: Get your pixel key from your Tamtam dashboard, Pixel tab.
quickstart.step2Warning: Your keys always start with tmsk_. Never commit your key in your source code. Use an environment variable.
quickstart.step3Desc: Three standard events, ready to use:
quickstart.step3DoneDesc: Events appear in your Tamtam dashboard, Pixel, Live Events within 30 seconds of being sent.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.card1Desc: Events are validated server-side before recording. IP, device fingerprint, rate limiting — all handled before a single event reaches your dashboard.
howItWorks.card2Desc: Every Tamtam click appends ?tm_ref=xxx to your URL. The Pixel reads this parameter automatically and links the conversion back to the correct Echo.
howItWorks.card3Desc: Per-key rate limiting (100 req/min). Per-IP rate limiting (200 req/min). Failed auth lockout (10 attempts, 15min block).

tmRef.title: tm_ref lifecycle
tmRef.desc: The tm_ref parameter is Tamtam's attribution key. It links each conversion to the Echo who shared the link.
tmRef.step1Desc: Each Echo receives a unique link with their personal tm_ref.
tmRef.step2Desc: The visitor arrives on your site with the tm_ref in the URL.
tmRef.step3Desc: The JavaScript SDK automatically reads the URL parameter and persists it in localStorage.
tmRef.step4Desc: You fire an event — the tm_ref is included automatically.
tmRef.step5Desc: Tamtam attributes the conversion to the correct Echo and updates the dashboard in real time.
tmRef.infoTitle: Each Echo has a unique tm_ref
tmRef.infoDesc: Two Echos sharing the same campaign will have different tm_refs. Attribution is always precise, even if thousands of Echos share the same campaign.

api.title: API Reference
api.authDesc: All requests require this header. Keys are managed in Dashboard, Pixel tab.
api.pixelVsTmrefTitle: Pixel ID vs tm_ref — don't confuse them
api.pixelIdDesc: Identifies your account / pixel. Used in the X-Tamtam-Key header. One per pixel, created in your dashboard. Secret — never expose client-side.
api.tmRefDesc: Identifies which Echo referred the visitor. Auto-added to URLs when an Echo shares your campaign. Public — visible in the URL. Automatically read by the JS SDK.
api.paramEvent: Event name: page_view, sign_up, activation, purchase, or custom
api.paramTmRef: Attribution reference from URL param (auto-read by JS SDK)
api.paramValue: Monetary value of the event in XOF
api.errorCampaignNotFound: tm_ref doesn't match an active campaign
api.endpoint2Desc: Test your connection. No authentication required.
api.customEventsNote: Custom events supported — use any lowercase snake_case string.

examples.title: Integration examples
examples.tabEcommerceDesc: Track purchases on a Senegalese e-commerce site. Fire activation on order confirmation.
examples.tabMobileDesc: Track installs and signups from a mobile app. The Pixel works via your backend — no client-side SDK needed.
examples.tabLeadGenDesc: Track form submissions on a landing page.
examples.tabSaasDesc: Track signups and first meaningful action (activation). Recommended: fire activation on first project created or first team invite.
examples.tabReactNativeDesc: Capture the tm_ref from a deep link and persist it with AsyncStorage for post-install attribution.
examples.tabMobileWebDesc: On mobile web, persist the tm_ref in localStorage with a 7-day window to handle cross-page navigations.
examples.tabMobileWebWarning: The JS SDK already handles this persistence automatically on desktop. This pattern is useful for PWAs or mobile sites without the JS SDK.

testing.title: Test your integration
testing.step3Title: Check Live Events in your dashboard
testing.issue1Q: Event sent but not appearing in dashboard
testing.issue1A: Check that your pixel key is active in Dashboard, Pixel tab. Inactive keys return 200 but events are silently dropped.
testing.issue2A: Your IP may have been flagged. Wait 15 minutes. If persistent, contact contact@tamma.me with your pixel ID.
testing.issue3A: The tm_ref param must be present in the URL when the page loads. Check that your redirect preserves query parameters. Example: yoursite.com/signup?tm_ref=abc123
testing.issue4A: Use event_id with a stable unique value (order ID, user ID). Tamtam deduplicates events with the same event_id within 24h.
testing.benchmarkNoteDesc: The JS SDK sends events in the background via navigator.sendBeacon or async fetch. Tracking never impacts your site's performance — no blocked rendering, no visible delay for the user.

privacy.title: Privacy & data
privacy.card1Item1: The event name and value you send
privacy.card1Item4: User agent / device fingerprint (fraud scoring only)
privacy.card2Desc: If you pass user_data, hash it first:
privacy.card2Note: Plain-text PII is stripped server-side before storage. Never pass raw emails, names, or phone numbers.
privacy.card3Content1: Tamtam is compliant with Senegal's loi 2008-12 on personal data protection (CDP Senegal).
privacy.card3Content2: For EU visitors, the Pixel qualifies as a necessary analytics cookie — no consent banner required for basic click tracking.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: Does the Pixel work server-side or client-side?
faq.a1: Both. The JavaScript SDK works client-side (browser). For maximum accuracy (no adblockers), use server-side tracking via the REST API directly from your backend.
faq.q2: How do I handle cookie consent?
faq.a2: The basic Pixel (page_view) does not require explicit consent under Senegalese law. For conversion events, wait for user consent before calling tamtam('track', ...).
faq.q3: Can I use a single Pixel for multiple domains?
faq.a3: Yes. Create one Pixel per campaign (not per domain) from your dashboard. A single key can track multiple subdomains.
faq.a4: The /api/pixel/event endpoint targets a p95 < 200ms. Events appear in the dashboard in < 30s. Status page: status.tamma.me (coming soon).
faq.q5: Does the Pixel work with an ad blocker?
faq.a5: Ad blockers may block the JavaScript SDK. For critical conversions (sign_up, purchase), use server-side tracking — it is not affected by ad blockers.
faq.q6: How do I delete test events?
faq.a6: In Dashboard, Pixel, your pixel, Settings, enable "Test mode" to mark events as test. Test events are excluded from campaign metrics.
faq.q7: Does each Echo have a unique tm_ref?
faq.a7: Yes. The tm_ref is unique per Echo and per campaign. If two Echos share the same campaign, each visitor will arrive with a different tm_ref, allowing precise attribution to the correct Echo. The tm_ref is generated automatically by Tamtam — you don't need to configure anything.
faq.q8: How do I persist the tm_ref on mobile?
faq.a8: On mobile web, the JS SDK automatically persists the tm_ref in localStorage with a 7-day window. On React Native, capture the tm_ref from the initial deep link with Linking.getInitialURL() and store it via AsyncStorage. For PWAs without the JS SDK, use the localStorage pattern described in the Examples, Mobile Web section.

cta.title: Ready to integrate?
cta.card1Desc: Create your Tamtam account and generate your first Pixel key in 2 minutes.
cta.card2Desc: Manage your Pixels, view your events in real time.
cta.card3Desc: Our team responds within 24h to help you with integration.
